bugs.root_cause: CleanStale used syscall.Stat_t and os.Getuid inline. syscall.Stat_t is not defined on Windows, so cross-compilation for GOOS=windows fails at the build step. Local `go build` on Linux compiled fine and hid the problem; the release workflow cross-compiles for six targets and caught it.
bugs.fix: Extracted the ownership check into a platform-specific helper isOwnedByCurrentUser(info). Added cli/internal/sandbox/ownership_unix.go (build tag !windows) with the real syscall.Stat_t/os.Getuid check, and cli/internal/sandbox/ownership_windows.go (build tag windows) that returns true (Windows uses per-user temp paths by default). Removed syscall import from staging.go.

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: Issued should be Revoked
Wrong reference: resolveSigningProfile should be resolveSigningProfileForSign
Wrong reference: protorekorv1 should be protorekor
Multi-provider loadout apply fails: 'concise-comments not found' for gemini-cli even though content/rules/gemini-cli/concise-comments/ exists on disk
bugs.root_cause: applyPrecedence used (name, type) as dedup key for all types. Same-named rules for different providers all hashed to the same key; only the alphabetically-first provider (claude-code) survived, others went to Overridden.
bugs.fix: Include provider in dedup key for all non-universal types (Rules, Hooks, Commands, Loadouts). Universal types keep empty provider key since they are provider-agnostic.

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: AnonID should be AnonymousID
Wrong reference: renderPlaceholder should be renderConfigContent
Hooks silently fail to fire after syllago install — Claude Code reads hooks.PreToolUse but syllago injects hooks.before_tool_execute
bugs.root_cause: installHook, uninstallHook, and checkHookStatus used parseHookFile's returned event directly as an sjson key. hook.json stores the canonical event name (before_tool_execute) but provider settings.json uses provider-native keys (PreToolUse for Claude Code). No translation happened, so the hook landed under a key the provider never reads. IsValidHookEvent accepted both canonical and native names, so validation didn't catch it.
bugs.fix: Added converter.TranslateHookEvent(event, prov.Slug) after validation in all three functions. If translation succeeds (canonical → native), use the translated key; otherwise leave as-is (event was already native or provider-specific).

bugs.error_message: TUI 'Add from provider' for hooks wrote entire settings.json into library as hook.json; later install failed with 'hook file missing event field'
bugs.root_cause: discoverHooksFromProvider set addDiscoveryItem.path = loc.Path (the settings.json path). addSingleItem routed through add.AddItems → add.writeItem which os.ReadFile's item.Path and writes it as hook.json. The whole settings.json ({"hooks": {"PreToolUse": [...]}}) ended up as the library hook.json, which has no top-level event field.
bugs.fix: Added hookData (*converter.HookData) and hookSourceDir (directory of settings.json) fields to addDiscoveryItem. Populated in discoverHooksFromProvider from the already-parsed HookData. addSingleItem now branches on itemType==Hooks && hookData!=nil and calls a new writeHookToLibrary helper that json.MarshalIndents HookData → hook.json, bundles scripts via converter.BundleHookScripts, and writes .syllago.yaml metadata. Mirrors cli/cmd/syllago/add_cmd.go addHooksFromLocation.

bugs.fix: Rewrote 8→14 lines (2 removed) | Also: // mergeConfirmIntoDiscovery appends user-selected; // list before entering Review. Safe to call multi

bugs.error_message: panic: runtime error: index out of range [86] with length 85 at cli/internal/tui/add_wizard.go:714 in mergeConfirmIntoDiscovery
bugs.root_cause: mergeConfirmIntoDiscovery appended user-selected confirm items to the END of discoveredItems (after the installed block), breaking the [actionable][installed] layout invariant. When showInstalled=false and installedCount>0, visibleDiscoveryItems() returns discoveredItems[:actionableCount] and buildDiscoveryList builds a checkboxList sized to visible items only (actionableCount). The subsequent explicit selection loop indexed selected[] from actionableCount+installedCount up to len(discoveredItems), which is out of range when installedCount>0. Also: the explicit selection loop was redundant — buildDiscoveryList already pre-selects StatusNew items (which merged items are).
bugs.fix: Added preMergeActionableCount/preMergeInstalledCount fields captured once in handleDiscoveryDone. Restructured mergeConfirmIntoDiscovery to (a) split current slice back to origActionable/origInstalled using the pre-merge baselines, (b) rebuild as [origActionable + merged, origInstalled] so merged items live in the actionable block, (c) update actionableCount = baseActionable + len(merged), (d) remove redundant explicit selection loop — let buildDiscoveryList's StatusNew pre-selection cover merged items. Added regression test TestAddWizard_MergePreservesLayoutWithInstalled.

bugs.error_message: Triage preview blank for all content types; MCP shows path traversal: .cloud.json escapes .; hooks/commands not going to drill-in; confidence ratings missing in discovery left pane
bugs.root_cause: addConfirmItem was missing hookData/hookSourceDir/catalogItem fields that downstream code (loadTriagePreview, enterReviewDrillIn) needs. Single-file items from nativeItemsToDiscovery and DiscoverFromProvider have SourceDir empty; handleDiscoveryDone stored empty sourceDir on addConfirmItem. discoverFromProvider set ci.Path to file path instead of parent dir for single-file items. Pattern-detected items had tier=empty so confidence ratings showed as muted invisible dots.
bugs.fix: Added hookData/hookSourceDir/catalogItem fields to addConfirmItem. handleDiscoveryDone derives sourceDir from filepath.Dir(path) when empty, copies hookData/hookSourceDir/catalogItem, defaults tier to TierHigh for non-analyzer items. mergeConfirmIntoDiscovery preserves hookData and prefers original catalogItem. loadTriagePreview handles hooks via hookData virtual rendering. discoverFromProvider builds ci.Path=filepath.Dir(d.Path) for single-file items.

bugs.error_message: MCP drill-in shows entire settings file (~10,000 lines) instead of just the relevant server snippet
bugs.root_cause: enterReviewDrillIn checked item.catalogItem != nil before the MCP extractJSONSection branch. mergeConfirmIntoDiscovery always synthesizes a catalogItem, so the MCP branch was unreachable.
bugs.fix: Moved MCP extractJSONSection check BEFORE catalogItem check in enterReviewDrillIn.

bugs.error_message: Skills/agents drill-in shows No previewable file found and left pane shows only a dot
bugs.root_cause: scanDrillInFiles used filepath.WalkDir on a symlinked root. WalkDir uses Lstat internally so symlinked dir appears as non-directory entry; filepath.Rel returns . producing files=[.], which PrimaryFileName cannot match.
bugs.fix: Resolve root path via filepath.EvalSymlinks before filepath.WalkDir.

bugs.error_message: Zero previews on triage/discovery step for symlinked content types
bugs.root_cause: loadTriagePreview called catalog.SafeResolve which rejects symlinks (EvalSymlinks + containment check). For symlinked skills, resolved path escapes baseDir so preview returns empty.
bugs.fix: Removed SafeResolve from loadTriagePreview; ReadFileContent string-prefix guard is symlink-safe. Added MCP extraction via extractJSONSection.

bugs.error_message: Registry removal shows success toast but registries remain visible after removal
bugs.root_cause: doRegistryRemoveCmd always wrote to ~/.syllago/config.json (global) but LoadAndScan merges three config sources: global + contentRoot + projectRoot. When running syllago from a directory like ~/.config/pai/, registries defined in that dir's .syllago/config.json survive global-only deletion and reappear after rescanCatalog.
bugs.fix: Capture a.contentRoot and a.projectRoot in the closure. Before writing global config, iterate all project-local config files and remove the named registry from each that contains it.

bugs.error_message: CATALOG_002: parsing .syllago.yaml: yaml: unmarshal errors: line 8: cannot unmarshal !!map into string
bugs.root_cause: Schema collision: rulestore (D11/D13) writes nested `source:` map with provider/scope/path/format/filename/hash/split_method/captured_at, but the Meta struct used by catalog scanning declares `Source string` (legacy flat shape). yaml.Unmarshal into Meta fails type-check on the map, so any rulestore-written rule fails catalog load — breaking TUI rescan and any subsequent `syllago add`.
bugs.fix: Made Load() dual-shape: on Meta unmarshal failure, retry as RuleMetadata; if Type=="rule" succeeds, project into Meta via new ruleMetadataToMeta() helper. Non-rule parse errors pass through unchanged. Added TestLoad_AcceptsRuleMetadataShape regression test.

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: runMOATSync should be func
Trust: Unknown persists after registry add and successful sync
bugs.root_cause: moat.Sync discarded verified bundleBytes; neither CLI dispatcher nor TUI sync handler wrote <cacheDir>/moat/registries/<name>/{manifest.json,signature.bundle}. EnrichFromMOATManifests always observed 'MOAT cache missing' and skipped enrichment, so trust stayed Unknown indefinitely.
bugs.fix: Added BundleBytes to moat.SyncResult and populated after fetchBundleBytes. Added moat.WriteManifestCache (atomic write-then-rename of both files) + atomicWriteFile helper. CLI dispatcher (syncMOATRegistry) and TUI orchestrator (runMOATSync) now call it on every successful non-304 sync. New TUI surfaces: moat_sync.go (orchestrator, indirection seam), tofu_modal.go (inline TOFU approval so unpinned-profile syncs can be accepted without leaving the TUI). handleSync routes MOAT vs git; handleTOFUResult re-syncs with acceptTOFU=true on accept.

bugs.error_message: User: 'I just added [a registry], and it still says trust unknown.' Manifest cache writer (bug-433) only ran on Sync action, not on Add.
bugs.root_cause: handleRegistryAddDone only toasted + rescanned. doRegistryAddCmd correctly set Type=moat for allowlist/self-declared registries, but no sync was dispatched, so the manifest cache stayed empty and EnrichFromMOATManifests downgraded trust to Unknown on the next rescan. The fix path for bug-433 (writing the cache) was correct but only kicked in for the explicit Sync action.
bugs.fix: Added isMOAT field to registryAddDoneMsg; when true, handleRegistryAddDone keeps registryOpInProgress=true and dispatches doMOATSyncCmd(name, false). The sync's own handler triggers a rescan after cache is populated. Self-declared (unpinned) registries surface the existing TOFU modal — the correct first-add consent gate.

bugs.error_message: User: 'Esc to exit a drill-in on a registry card. You can only use Q to go back to the main registry card view.'
bugs.root_cause: Only the keyQuit ('q') case in app_update.go handled gallery drill-in back-out. KeyEsc fell through to routeKey, which delegated to the gallery model — gallery's Esc handler only clears search filter, never exits drill-in. Esc-on-drill-in had no path back to the gallery.
bugs.fix: Extracted handleBackOut(includeTabReset bool) so keyQuit and KeyEsc share back-out semantics. Esc passes false (no tab reset) so it can still fall through to routeKey for clearing persisted search filters on Content/Registries tabs. q passes true (matches historical 'q from non-library tab → Library' behavior).

bugs.error_message: User: 'I don't see the registry in the TUI, but when I try to add it, it says it already exists.'
bugs.root_cause: LoadAndScan and the TUI startup path both filtered registries through registry.IsCloned(name), which checks ~/.syllago/registries/<name>/. MOAT registries cache at <cacheDir>/moat/registries/<name>/ and are never git-cloned, so IsCloned was always false for them. Result: configured MOAT registries were absent from RegistrySources, so the TUI gallery showed them as 'No items found' — but config-membership checks (e.g. registry add's 'already exists') still saw them, leaving the user confused.
bugs.fix: In both LoadAndScan and the TUI startup, treat MOAT registries as 'always include' with Path = <cacheDir>/moat/registries/<name>. Git registries keep the IsCloned guard. Added regression test TestLoadAndScan_IncludesUnsynced_MOAT pinning the new contract.

bugs.error_message: Add failed: git clone failed: fatal: destination path '/home/hhewett/.syllago/registries/OpenScribbler/syllago-meta-registry' already exists and is not an empty directory.
bugs.root_cause: registry.Clone had no pre-flight check for an existing destination. When git clone failed with 'destination already exists', the cleanup branch unconditionally ran os.RemoveAll(dir) — destroying any pre-existing user content at that path. Worse, the error message gave no hint how to recover. A stale clone (e.g. left over from a previous failed registry add where config.yaml was never persisted) couldn't be reused and was silently wiped on the retry.
bugs.fix: Stat the destination before invoking git. If it exists, return an actionable error (`syllago registry remove <name>` or delete the directory) WITHOUT touching the existing dir. The os.RemoveAll cleanup now only runs after the pre-flight stat has confirmed the dir didn't exist at function entry, so it can never destroy unrelated data. Added regression test TestIntegration_StaleClonePreserved that pre-creates a marker file in the clone dir and asserts both that Clone fails and that the marker survives.

bugs.error_message: Sync failed: MOAT_IDENTITY_MISMATCH: building certificate identity matcher: when verifying a certificate identity, there must be subject alternative name criteria
bugs.root_cause: VerifyManifest and VerifyItemSigstore both called sigstore-go's verify.NewShortCertificateIdentity(issuer, issuerRegex, sanValue, sanRegex) with hardcoded empty strings for issuerRegex and sanRegex. The bundled allowlist entry for OpenScribbler/syllago-meta-registry pins via subject_regex (no literal subject) so it can cover three workflow paths (moat.yml | moat-publisher.yml | moat-registry.yml). With Subject empty and SubjectRegex ignored, sigstore-go received no SAN criteria and rejected the matcher constructor. The struct comment in config.SigningProfile reinforced the mistake by claiming subject_regex was 'reserved for slice 2+'.
bugs.fix: Forward pinnedProfile.IssuerRegex and pinnedProfile.SubjectRegex through to NewShortCertificateIdentity at both call sites. Updated the SigningProfile godoc to reflect actual behavior. Added regression tests TestVerifyManifest_HappyPath_SubjectRegex (regex-only profile verifies the real Phase 0 fixture) and TestVerifyManifest_WrongSubjectRegex (regex that does not cover the SAN is rejected) so the regex path stays exercised end-to-end.

bugs.error_message: Sync rejected: signing profile changed for OpenScribbler/syllago-meta-registry. Remove and re-add the registry to re-approve.
bugs.root_cause: SigningProfile.Equal() compared literal Subject only. The bundled allowlist (signing_identities.json) pins regex form (Subject="" + SubjectRegex set) to span multiple workflow paths under one trust grant; wire manifests typically declare a single literal Subject. Equal returned false → NeedsSigningProfileReapproval returned true → Sync rejected on every sync of regex-pinned registries.
bugs.fix: Added SigningProfile.AuthorizesIdentity() — regex-aware authorization check used by NeedsSigningProfileReapproval. When pinned uses regex form, the regex matches incoming literal Subject (and IssuerRegex similarly). Numeric IDs (RepositoryID, RepositoryOwnerID) still match exactly per ADR 0007 (the cryptographic anchor against repo-transfer forgery is not relaxed). Equal kept strict for callers that need literal comparison.
Rewrote 7→3 lines (4 removed) | Also: root, err := findContentRepoRoot(); cfg, err := config.Load(root)

bugs.error_message: TUI gallery shows '0 items' for MOAT registry after add+sync; no content visible in library/explorer either
bugs.root_cause: MOAT registry cache dirs hold only manifest.json + signature.bundle (no skills/, rules/, etc. content tree). LoadAndScan points the catalog scanner at <cacheDir>/moat/registries/<name>/, where scanRoot finds zero content type directories and returns 0 items. EnrichCatalog only mutates pre-existing cat.Items, so it never adds the manifest entries either. Result: cat.ByRegistry(name) returns empty, gallery card reads '0 items', library/explorer never see the registry rows.
bugs.fix: Added materializeMOATItems(cat, regName, m) in cli/internal/moat/enrich.go that synthesizes one ContentItem per manifest entry (Path empty since blob is fetched at install time, Type via FromMOATType, Source/Registry tagged). Called from EnrichFromMOATManifests BEFORE EnrichCatalog and attachRegistryTrust so trust enrichment hits the new rows and TotalItems counts include them. Idempotent on (Type,Name) so future pre-staged content won't duplicate. Skips MOAT-unknown types (hooks/mcp) per spec.

bugs.error_message: After successful MOAT sync, registry card glyph stays orange '!' (stale) and contents-sidebar status renders 'fresh' in danger-red instead of 'Fresh' in muted color.
bugs.root_cause: attachRegistryTrust wrote rt.Staleness = status.String() which returns lowercase ('fresh'/'stale'/'expired'), but the catalog.RegistryTrust.Staleness contract documented in catalog/types.go is title-case ('Fresh'/'Stale'/'Expired'/'Missing'). All TUI consumers compare against capital 'Fresh' so every fresh registry was misread as stale. Producer tests didn't catch it because they only assert on warnings, never on the rt.Staleness field. TUI tests construct fixtures with capital strings so they never exercised the producer's lowercase output.
bugs.fix: Added StalenessStatus.CatalogLabel() returning title-case strings; updated attachRegistryTrust to use CatalogLabel() for the catalog field while String() stays lowercase for diagnostic warnings. Added regression test TestAttachRegistryTrust_StalenessLabelIsCatalogContract pinning the wire label.
Rewrote 40→89 lines (11 removed) | Also: // Lockfile: UNSIGNED → null bundle. Clock seam sh; // path so tests can pin pinned_at deterministical

bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: Error should be Errorf

bugs.root_cause: Function uses await but wasn't declared async

bugs.error_message: wizard invariant: installStepProvider entered with empty item — TUI panics on first Update message after [i] opens install wizard for an unstaged MOAT (registry) item; also no [i] Install button in metadata bar for those items.
bugs.root_cause: syllago-kdxus added a doMOATInstallCmd path so unstaged MOAT items (Path=='', Source==registryName) can be installed via the TUI, but three pre-existing gatekeepers were not updated: (1) installWizardModel.validateStep() at install.go:240 panicked on m.item.Path=='', (2) computeMetaPanelData in metapanel.go:53 hid the Install button when item.Library==false && item.Registry!='', (3) library.go:723 had a duplicate canInstall computation with the same stale check.
bugs.fix: validateStep now treats an item as valid when Name is set AND (Path or Source is set) — unstaged MOAT items have Source set and qualify. Both canInstall sites accept items where isUnstagedRegistryItem(item) is true. Added TestInstallWizard_ValidateStep_AcceptsUnstagedMOATItem regression test.

bugs.error_message: TUI install toast collapses output.StructuredError to '[CODE] message', dropping the Details/Suggestion fields. Concretely: '[MOAT_004] attestation verification failed for OpenScribbler/syllago-meta-registry/split-rules-llm' was the entire toast — the actual cause (MOAT_IDENTITY_MISMATCH cert subject vs pinned profile) was unreachable without a debug build.
bugs.root_cause: StructuredError.Error() only formats Code+Message by design (CLI users get full details via PrintStructuredError, not Error()). The TUI toast handler called msg.err.Error() directly, so anything wrapped in NewStructuredErrorDetail lost the diagnostic content the CLI surfaces.
bugs.fix: Added formatToastErr(err) helper that errors.As to output.StructuredError and appends Details when present. Used it in handleInstallDone. Toast still truncates the on-screen line, but [c] copies the full message to the clipboard, so users can paste the diagnostic.
